channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: No longer taken advantage of: Trump signs order prioritizing 'unified' US foreign policy front
item.description: A new executive order emphasizes the State Department's responsibility to uphold President Donald Trump's foreign policy priorities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: McConnell only Republican to vote against Gabbard
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) on Wednesday voted against confirming former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii) to serve as the nation’s top intelligence official, sending a strong message to fellow GOP senators, some of whom privately doubted her qualifications to hold the job.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Durbin calls for investigation into Patel's role in FBI firings
item.description: Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.) is asking the Department of Justice’s (DOJ) inspector general to investigate “highly credible information” that Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to head the FBI, is already directing a “purge” of the bureau before he’s been confirmed by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Durbin calls for investigation into Patel's role in FBI firings
item.description: Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.) is asking the Department of Justice’s (DOJ) inspector general to investigate “highly credible information” that Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to head the FBI, is already directing a “purge” of the bureau before he’s been confirmed by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Minneapolis moves forward on proposal to shift violence prevention programs to the county
item.description: After a long and sometimes heated discussion Monday afternoon, the Minneapolis City Council agreed to move forward with a controversial proposal to shift around $1.2 million to Hennepin County so that it can temporarily take over the management of two city violence prevention programs.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Medical field needs more training like cultural competency, not less, Nevada lawmakers argue
item.description: A proposal to encourage — not require — medical practitioners to get training in subjects ranging from suicide prevention to cultural competency drew an extremely cold reaction from lawmakers on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Top Justice Department official orders prosecutors to drop charges against NYC Mayor Eric Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Justice Department on Monday ordered federal prosecutors to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams, arguing in a remarkable departure from longstanding norms that the case was interfering with the mayor’s ability to aid the president’s crackdown on illegal
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: New felony count added to charges against Sen. Nicole Mitchell for April 2024 incident
item.description: Becker County Attorney Brian McDonald added a second burglary charge Monday against state Sen. Nicole Mitchell, DFL-Woodbury, stemming from an incident in April 2024 in Detroit Lakes. Both charges are felonies.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Mathew Holding Eagle III, MPR News

item.title: Congress now has a DOGE Caucus. Here's how its co-chair says it will work
item.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Rep. Pete Sessions, co-chair of the House DOGE Caucus, on how he plans to work with the Department of Government Efficiency.
item.dc:creator: Mary Louise Kelly, Kai McNamee, and Justine Kenin, MPR News

item.title: Trump fires Office of Government Ethics chief
item.description: President Trump has fired the director of the Office of Government Ethics, the agency announced Monday. The office posted on its website that it had been notified Trump was removing David Huitema, who had been nominated by former President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: U.S. judge keeps his block on Trump federal buyout plan in place for now
item.description: A U.S. judge kept his block on President Donald Trump’s buyout plan for federal employees in place on Monday and said he would consider imposing it for a longer period of time.
item.dc:creator: MPR News

item.title: HIV infections could jump over 6 times if US support dropped & not replaced, UNAIDS chief says
item.description: The head of the U.N. AIDS agency said Monday the number of new HIV infections could jump more than six times by 2029 if American support of the biggest AIDS program is dropped, warning that millions of people could die and more resistant strains of the disease could emerge.
item.dc:creator: MARIA CHENG AP Medical Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Plan to break up Nevada's biggest agency could bring lower health care costs
item.description: Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo is no fan of big government, and a plan he announced in January's State of the State address takes aim at Nevada's biggest agency, the Department of Health and Human Services.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump signs order to establish White House Faith Office
item.description: Trump directed the office to identify ways to reduce burdens on the free exercise of religion and direct all federal agencies to designate a “faith liaison” within 90 days.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: West Virginia leads 22 states suing over New York Climate Change Superfund Act
item.description: West Virginia led the charge against New York’s Climate Change Superfund Act, suing to block the law.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, Fox 5

item.title: Tampa Bay lawmaker files bill to allow state funds to be invested in Bitcoin
item.description: A Tampa Bay lawmaker filed a bill on Friday that would allow Florida's chief financial officer to invest state funds in Bitcoin.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he'll sign order promoting plastic straws
item.description: President Donald Trump on Friday said he plans to sign an executive order promoting plastic straws after his predecessor sought to phase out single-use plastics from government operations.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Tampa Bay lawmaker pushes bold legislation to protect homeownership from corporations
item.description: In what's being called a "bold new measure," one Tampa Bay lawmaker wants to put families over corporate elites by making sure local Floridians can stay in their neighborhoods.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
